dimensions.rationale: The receipt is bound to the exact candidate and reports LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY, zero remote writes, and no protected execution or sensitive data.

The command receipt and provenance chain are substantially candidate-bound, but the post-review drift check omits the effect-bearing production workflow and other reviewed tooling, so final delivery integrity is bypassable.
Local and protected limitations are stated honestly, but the validator can still report closure after an unreviewed post-candidate change to the protected production workflow.
The reviewed execution was local and synthetic with zero remote writes or protected effects, so no external compensating rollback is evidenced as necessary.

findings.finding: Candidate, request, reply, model-family, task-ID, and distinct-execution bindings are now mechanically represented and checked, with platform identity explicitly limited to the retained Codex audit log.
findings.resolution_ref: The v2 provenance schema and assurance validator hash-bind both attempts and enforce exact model/task identities, a shared candidate/request/receipt, distinct tasks, and distinct final replies.

findings.finding: The local gate evidence is now candidate-bound and preserves exact command identities, exit codes, tool versions, raw-output metadata, sanitized outputs, and verified sanitized-output digests.
findings.resolution_ref: The exact receipt hash matches a 15-command LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY record, and the validator enforces complete unique command closure plus sanitized-output digest and leakage checks.

findings.finding: Write authorization now includes prospective repository, LFS, object, and release-asset demand and reserves cumulative demand before invoking effectful callbacks.
findings.resolution_ref: CapacityDemand projection, mandatory non-empty projected-write demand, cumulative reservation, and boundary-crossing tests close the prospective-capacity bypass.

findings.finding: Post-review mechanical closure is bypassable: PROTECTED_AFTER_REVIEW_PREFIXES covers only .github/workflows/moomooau-stage6- while the candidate's protected production workflow is .github/workflows/moomooau-production.yml; capture_candidate_gates.py is also outside the protected set. A later delivery commit can alter these reviewed effect/evidence surfaces without causing the assurance validator to block.
findings.required_fix: Expand post-review drift protection to every reviewed/effect-bearing workflow and evidence-capture tool, add regression tests proving such post-candidate mutations block closure, then issue a new immutable candidate/request/receipt and repeat both independent re-reviews.

limitations: This read-only review did not execute tests, validators, workflows, network calls, credentials, protected Oracles, Gmail, or private-repository operations.
The receipt proves only retained local synthetic evidence; repository hashes and task IDs require the Codex thread audit log for platform execution identity.
No production health, final AC-033, deployment, publication, or readiness for RMD-06/RMD-07 is inferred.
summary: FAIL: the three applicable initial findings are resolved, but the required post-candidate closure is bypassable because material production workflow and evidence-capture drift is not protected.
